pages.text: The path from Uranium Ore to Reactor Fuel involves several chemical steps:$(br2)1. Yellowcake + Nitric Acid -> Uranyl Nitrate$(br)2. Uranyl Nitrate -> Uranium Dioxide (UO2)$(br)3. UO2 + Hydrofluoric Acid -> Uranium Tetrafluoride (UF4)$(br)4. UF4 + Fluorine Gas -> Uranium Hexafluoride (UF6)

Each of these steps occurs in the Chemical Converter. $(br2)UF6 is the gaseous form required for enrichment in the Centrifuge. After enrichment, UF6 must be converted back to UO2 pellets for use in fuel rods.
